summary: react-simple-utils-kit postinstall script exfiltrates host data and executes remote commands
details: The package ships a postinstall.js that runs automatically on `npm install`. The script imports child_process and performs multiple outbound POST requests, a shape consistent with host reconnaissance and command-and-control beaconing rather than any documented utility-library purpose. The package name ("react-simple-utils-kit") advertises a benign React helper library and gives no legitimate reason to spawn child processes or contact external endpoints during installation. Installing this package causes the postinstall hook to execute on the developer/build machine before any code is consciously imported, putting environment variables, host identifiers, and shell-execution capability in the hands of the postinstall destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
